# ValueSnap Appraisal - PWA Implementation Guide

## 🎯 Overview

This guide covers the Progressive Web App (PWA) implementation of ValueSnap Appraisal, transforming the existing React Native app into a production-ready web application with offline support, authentication, and real-time features.

## ✨ New Features

### 1. **PWA Capabilities**
- ✅ Installable on desktop and mobile
- ✅ Offline support with service workers
- ✅ App-like experience (standalone mode)
- ✅ Install prompts and banners
- ✅ Cached assets for fast loading

### 2. **Enhanced Backend API v1**
- ✅ Versioned REST API (`/api/v1`)
- ✅ JWT authentication (guest sessions)
- ✅ Rate limiting (protection against abuse)
- ✅ Better error handling
- ✅ Structured logging
- ✅ Backward compatible with legacy endpoints

### 3. **Authentication System**
- ✅ Guest sessions (no signup required)
- ✅ JWT tokens with auto-refresh
- ✅ Secure token storage
- ✅ Session management

## 🚀 Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Backend dependencies
cd backend
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Frontend dependencies
cd ../appraisal-mobile
npm install
```

### Environment Configuration

Update your `.env` file:

```bash
# Backend API
BACKEND_HOST=0.0.0.0
BACKEND_PORT=5000
BACKEND_DEBUG=false

# Security
SECRET_KEY=your-secret-key-here
JWT_SECRET=your-jwt-secret-here
JWT_EXPIRATION_HOURS=24

# Rate Limiting
RATELIMIT_ENABLED=true
RATELIMIT_DEFAULT=100 per hour
RATELIMIT_STORAGE_URL=redis://localhost:6379  # or memory://

# CORS
CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:3000,https://yourdomain.com

# API Keys (existing)
OPENAI_API_KEY=your-openai-key
EBAY_SANDBOX_APP_ID=your-ebay-app-id
EBAY_SANDBOX_CERT_ID=your-ebay-cert-id
```

### Running the Application

#### Development Mode

**Backend (v1 API):**
```bash
cd backend
python run_backend_v1.py
```

**Frontend (PWA):**
```bash
cd appraisal-mobile
npm run web
```

#### Production Mode

**Backend:**
```bash
# Using Gunicorn (recommended)
cd backend
gunicorn -w 4 -b 0.0.0.0:5000 run_backend_v1:app

# Or with gevent for async
gunicorn -k gevent -w 4 -b 0.0.0.0:5000 run_backend_v1:app
```

**Frontend:**
```bash
cd appraisal-mobile
npm run build
npx serve -s build -l 3000
```

## 📱 PWA Features

### Service Worker

The service worker (`public/service-worker.js`) provides:
- **Offline support**: Cache static assets and API responses
- **Cache strategies**: 
  - Static assets: Cache-first
  - API requests: Network-first with cache fallback
- **Auto-updates**: Notifies users of new versions
- **Background sync**: Queue failed requests (future)

### Installation

Users can install the PWA:
1. **Desktop**: Click install icon in address bar or use banner
2. **Mobile**: "Add to Home Screen" prompt
3. **Programmatic**: Custom install banner in app

### Offline Experience

When offline, users can:
- View previously loaded pages
- See cached appraisals
- Queue new analyses for when connection returns
- Access the offline fallback page

## 🔐 Authentication Flow

### Guest Sessions (Default)

```javascript
import { createGuestSession, ensureSession } from './src/services/apiV1';

// Automatically create guest session on first use
await ensureSession();

// Or manually create
const session = await createGuestSession();
console.log(session.token, session.user_id);
```

### Using Authenticated API

```javascript
import { analyzeImage } from './src/services/apiV1';

// Automatically handles authentication
const result = await analyzeImage(imageBase64);
```

### Token Refresh

Tokens automatically refresh when expired:
- Guest tokens: 7 days
- Regular tokens: 24 hours (configurable)

## 🎨 API Endpoints

### V1 API (Recommended)

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Description |
|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| `/api/v1/health` | GET | No | Health check |
| `/api/v1/auth/guest` | POST | No | Create guest session |
| `/api/v1/analyze` | POST | Yes | Analyze item image |

### Legacy API (Backward Compatible)

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Description |
|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| `/health` | GET | No | Legacy health check |
| `/process` | POST | No | Legacy image processing |

### Example Requests

**Create Guest Session:**
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/v1/auth/guest
```

**Analyze Image:**
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/v1/analyze \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"image_base64": "base64_encoded_image"}'
```

## 🔧 Configuration

### Rate Limiting

Customize rate limits in `.env`:

```bash
# Global default
RATELIMIT_DEFAULT=100 per hour

# Use Redis for distributed rate limiting
RATELIMIT_STORAGE_URL=redis://localhost:6379

# Or in-memory for development
RATELIMIT_STORAGE_URL=memory://
```

### CORS

Configure allowed origins:

```bash
# Development
CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:3000,http://127.0.0.1:3000

# Production
CORS_ORIGINS=https://yourdomain.com,https://app.yourdomain.com

# Allow all (not recommended for production)
CORS_ORIGINS=*
```

## 📊 Monitoring

### Logging

Structured logs with levels:
```python
import logging

# Configure in .env
LOG_LEVEL=INFO  # DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL
```

### Health Check

Monitor service health:
```bash
curl http://localhost:5000/api/v1/health

# Response:
{
  "status": "healthy",
  "version": "v1",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-31T12:00:00",
  "services": {
    "openai": true,
    "ebay": true
  }
}
```

docker-compose up -d

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

### Service Worker Not Registering

```javascript
// Check browser console
if ('serviceWorker' in navigator) {
  console.log('Service workers are supported');
} else {
  console.log('Service workers are not supported');
}
```

### Token Issues

```javascript
import { clearAuth, createGuestSession } from './src/services/apiV1';

// Clear and recreate session
await clearAuth();
await createGuestSession();
```

### CORS Errors

Ensure backend CORS_ORIGINS includes your frontend URL.

================
File: pytest.ini
================
[pytest]
# Pytest Configuration

# Test discovery patterns
python_files = test_*.py *_test.py
python_classes = Test*
python_functions = test_*

# Test paths
testpaths = tests

# Output options
addopts = 
    -v
    --tb=short
    --strict-markers
    --disable-warnings
    -p no:warnings

# Markers for organizing tests
markers =
    unit: Unit tests (fast, no external dependencies)
    integration: Integration tests (may require backend server)
    slow: Slow-running tests
    backend: Tests that require backend server
    mock: Tests using mock APIs
    smoke: Smoke tests for quick validation
    cache: Tests for caching behavior and performance

# Logging
log_cli = false
log_cli_level = INFO
log_file = tests.log
log_file_level = DEBUG

# Coverage options (if using pytest-cov)
# addopts = --cov=src --cov=backend --cov-report=html --cov-report=term

# Asyncio configuration
asyncio_mode = auto

# Timeout for tests (requires pytest-timeout)
# timeout = 300

# Environment variables
env = 
    USE_MOCK=true
    CACHE_ENABLED=true
    LOG_LEVEL=INFO

================
File: README.md
================
# Estate Inventory Extraction System

🏠 **AI-powered estate inventory appraisal system** combining computer vision, GPT analysis, and eBay market research.

## 🚀 Quick Start

### Prerequisites
- Python 3.12+
- Node.js 20.x+ (for React Native mobile app)
- Docker & Docker Compose (for automated testing)

### Installation

1. **Clone the repository**
```bash
git clone https://github.com/emmanuellawal/ebay-extract.git
cd ebay-extract
```

2. **Set up Python environment**
```bash
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -r backend/requirements.txt
```

3. **Set up Mobile App (Optional)**
```bash
cd appraisal-mobile
npm install
```

4. **Configure environment variables**
```bash
cp .env.template .env
# Edit .env with your API keys (or leave USE_MOCK=true for spendless mode)
```

## 📱 Mobile App

The project includes a **React Native mobile app** built with Expo for iOS, Android, and Web.

### Quick Start - Mobile App

```bash
cd appraisal-mobile

# Run on web (easiest for testing)
npm run web

# Run on your phone with Expo Go
npm start
# Scan QR code with Expo Go app

# Run on Android emulator
npm run android

# Run on iOS simulator (macOS only)
npm run ios
```

**See [appraisal-mobile/README.md](appraisal-mobile/README.md) for detailed mobile app documentation.**

## � Docker Development (Recommended)

**NEW: Docker + ngrok hybrid architecture for seamless development!**

### Quick Start with Docker

```bash
# 1. Verify setup
./verify_docker_setup.sh

# 2. Start all services (frontend + backend)
docker-compose up --build

# 3. (Optional) Expose backend for eBay webhooks
ngrok http 5000
```

Or use the interactive menu:
```bash
./start.sh
```

### What You Get

- ✅ **One-command startup** - Both frontend and backend ready instantly
- ✅ **Container-to-container networking** - Fast, reliable internal communication
- ✅ **ngrok webhook support** - Production-ready eBay compliance
- ✅ **Automatic health checks** - Services start in correct order
- ✅ **Live reloading** - Code changes reflect immediately

### Access Points

| Service | URL | Purpose |
|---------|-----|---------|
| Frontend (Web) | http://localhost:19006 | React Native web interface |
| Backend API | http://localhost:5000 | Backend API endpoints |
| Backend Health | http://localhost:5000/health | Health check |
| ngrok Dashboard | http://127.0.0.1:4040 | ngrok web interface |

### Documentation

- **[DOCKER_NGROK_WORKFLOW.md](.github/instructions/DOCKER_NGROK_WORKFLOW.md)** - Complete workflow guide
- **[DOCKER_QUICK_REFERENCE.md](DOCKER_QUICK_REFERENCE.md)** - Quick command reference
- **[IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md](IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md)** - Architecture details

## �💰 Spendless Mode (Mock APIs)

**Perfect for development and testing without spending money on API calls!**

The system defaults to **mock mode** which simulates all API responses without making real calls to OpenAI or eBay:

```bash
# In your .env file:
USE_MOCK=true
CACHE_ENABLED=true
```

### Running in Mock Mode

**Option 1: Mock Backend Server**
```bash
python backend/run_mock_backend.py
```

**Option 2: Full Integration Test (Mock Mode)**
```bash
python final_integration_test.py
```

**Option 3: Pytest with Automatic Backend**
```bash
pytest tests/ -v -m mock
```

### Switching to Production Mode

When ready to use real APIs:

1. Get API keys:
   - OpenAI: https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
   - eBay Developer: https://developer.ebay.com/

2. Update `.env`:
```bash
USE_MOCK=false
OPENAI_API_KEY=your_actual_key_here
EBAY_SANDBOX_APP_ID=your_app_id
EBAY_SANDBOX_CERT_ID=your_cert_id
EBAY_SANDBOX_DEV_ID=your_dev_id
```

3. Run production backend:
```bash
python backend/run_backend.py
```

## 🧪 Testing

### Quick Start Testing

```bash
# Run all tests (recommended - automatic backend management)
pytest tests/ -v

# Run only fast tests
pytest tests/ -v -m smoke

# Generate coverage report
pytest tests/ --cov=src --cov=backend --cov-report=html
```

### Automated Testing with Docker

The easiest way to run tests with automatic backend management:

```bash
# Run all tests with Docker-managed backend
docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit

# Or run specific test service
docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build test-runner
```

### Comprehensive Test Suite

Tests automatically start/stop the backend server:

```bash
# Run all tests with verbose output
pytest tests/ -v

# Run specific test categories
pytest tests/ -v -m integration    # Backend API integration tests
pytest tests/ -v -m backend        # Backend-specific tests
pytest tests/ -v -m smoke          # Quick smoke tests
pytest tests/ -v -m mock           # Mock system tests (no API keys)
pytest tests/ -v -m cache          # Caching behavior tests

# Run specific test files
pytest tests/test_pipeline_smoke.py -v        # Pipeline smoke tests
pytest tests/test_pricing_rules.py -v         # Pricing and IQR tests
pytest tests/test_backend_integration.py -v   # Backend API tests
pytest tests/test_mock_system.py -v           # Mock system tests
pytest tests/test_edge_cases.py -v            # Edge cases and outliers
pytest tests/test_caching.py -v               # Cache functionality

# Run tests with coverage
pytest tests/ --cov=src --cov=backend --cov-report=html --cov-report=term

# Run tests and stop on first failure
pytest tests/ -v -x

# Run tests in parallel (faster)
pytest tests/ -v -n auto

# Run with detailed output
pytest tests/ -vv --tb=long
```

### End-to-End Integration Test

Full system integration test (MCP + Backend + Pipeline + Flutter):

```bash
# Comprehensive integration test with Docker backend
python final_integration_test.py

# Expected output: "ALL TESTS PASSED"
```

### Test Categories Explained

| Marker | Description | Speed |
|--------|-------------|-------|
| `smoke` | Quick validation tests | Fast (< 10s) |
| `integration` | Full API integration tests | Medium (30-60s) |
| `backend` | Backend-specific tests | Medium |
| `mock` | Mock system tests (no APIs) | Fast |
| `cache` | Cache behavior tests | Medium |
| `slow` | Long-running tests | Slow (> 60s) |

### Test Coverage Areas

✅ **Backend Integration** (`test_backend_integration.py`)
- Health check endpoint
- Image processing endpoint
- Error handling (missing data, invalid base64)
- CORS headers

✅ **Pipeline Smoke Tests** (`test_pipeline_smoke.py`)
- End-to-end pipeline execution
- Mock data processing
- Cache hit/miss behavior
- Output file generation

✅ **Pricing Rules** (`test_pricing_rules.py`)
- Price monotonicity (quick ≤ fair ≤ max)
- IQR-based pricing verification
- Fee and net proceeds calculations
- DOM (Days on Market) calculations

✅ **Mock System** (`test_mock_system.py`)
- Mock GPT interpreter
- Mock eBay fetcher
- Spendless mode validation
- Determinism and variety

✅ **Edge Cases** (`test_edge_cases.py`)
- No eBay matches found
- Single match scenarios
- Extreme outlier prices
- Invalid/corrupted images
- Zero and negative values
- Very large/tiny images

✅ **Caching** (`test_caching.py`)
- Cache hit on rerun
- Cache miss on new data
- Performance improvements
- Fingerprint consistency
- Cache storage format

### Running Specific Test Scenarios

```bash
# Test edge cases only
pytest tests/test_edge_cases.py::TestEdgeCases::test_no_ebay_matches_found -v

# Test pricing with outliers
pytest tests/test_edge_cases.py::TestPricingOutliers -v

# Test invalid images
pytest tests/test_edge_cases.py::TestInvalidImages -v

# Test cache performance
pytest tests/test_caching.py::test_cache_performance_improvement -v

# Test mock system variety
pytest tests/test_mock_system.py::test_mock_variety_of_items -v
```

### Continuous Integration

```bash
# CI-friendly test command (no interactive prompts)
pytest tests/ -v --tb=short --maxfail=3 --junit-xml=test-results.xml

# With coverage for CI
pytest tests/ --cov=src --cov=backend --cov-report=xml --cov-report=term
```

## 📁 Project Structure

```
ebay-extract/
├── appraisal_app/          # Flutter mobile app
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── main.dart       # Camera UI
│   │   └── api_client.dart # Backend integration
│   └── test/               # Flutter tests
├── backend/                # Python API server
│   ├── run_backend.py      # Production server
│   ├── run_mock_backend.py # Mock server (spendless)
│   ├── gpt_interpreter.py  # OpenAI integration
│   ├── ebay_client.py      # eBay API wrapper
│   ├── mock_*.py           # Mock implementations
│   └── requirements.txt
├── mcp_server/             # Model Context Protocol server
│   ├── ebay_mcp_server.py  # Enhanced eBay search
│   └── requirements.txt
├── src/estate_intake/      # Core processing pipeline
│   ├── config.py           # Global configuration
│   ├── pipeline.py         # Main workflow
│   ├── models.py           # Data models
│   └── pricing.py          # Pricing logic
├── tests/                  # Test suite
│   ├── conftest.py         # Pytest fixtures
│   ├── test_backend_integration.py
│   └── test_health_check.py
├── .env.template           # Environment template
├── .env                    # Your configuration (not in git)
├── Dockerfile.backend      # Backend container
├── docker-compose.test.yml # Test orchestration
├── pytest.ini              # Pytest configuration
└── final_integration_test.py # End-to-end test
```

## 🔧 Development Workflow

### 1. Start Backend Server

**Mock Mode (Spendless):**
```bash
python backend/run_mock_backend.py
```

**Production Mode:**
```bash
python backend/run_backend.py
```

### 2. Run Flutter App

```bash
cd appraisal_app
flutter pub get
flutter run
```

### 3. Process Test Cases

```bash
# Run the full pipeline on test cases
python src/estate_intake/pipeline.py
```

### 4. Run Tests

```bash
# Quick smoke tests
pytest tests/ -v -m smoke

# Full integration tests with auto backend
pytest tests/ -v -m integration

# Or use Docker for isolated environment
docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build
```

## 🌐 API Endpoints

### Backend Server (default: http://127.0.0.1:5000)

**Health Check**
```bash
GET /health

Response:
{
  "status": "healthy",
  "openai_configured": true,
  "ebay_configured": true
}
```

**Process Image**
```bash
POST /process
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "image_base64": "<base64_encoded_image>"
}

Response:
{
  "description": "...",
  "item_type": "...",
  "brand": "...",
  "search_keywords": "...",
  "total_found": 10,
  "quick_sell_price": 25.00,
  "patient_sell_price": 45.00
}
```

## 🔑 Environment Variables

See `.env.template` for all available options. Key variables:

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `USE_MOCK` | `true` | Enable mock/spendless mode |
| `CACHE_ENABLED` | `true` | Enable response caching |
| `BACKEND_HOST` | `0.0.0.0` | Backend server host |
| `BACKEND_PORT` | `5000` | Backend server port |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | - | OpenAI API key (production) |
| `OPENAI_MODEL` | `gpt-4o-mini` | GPT model to use |
| `EBAY_USE_SANDBOX` | `true` | Use eBay sandbox environment |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | `INFO` | Logging verbosity |

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

### Tests Failing: "Connection Refused"

The backend server isn't running. Use one of these solutions:

1. **Let pytest manage it** (recommended):
   ```bash
   pytest tests/ -v
   ```

2. **Use Docker** (most reliable):
   ```bash
   docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build
   ```

3. **Manually start backend first**:
   ```bash
   python backend/run_mock_backend.py
   # In another terminal:
   pytest tests/ -v
   ```

### Mock Mode Not Working

Ensure `.env` has:
```bash
USE_MOCK=true
```

Or set it explicitly:
```bash
USE_MOCK=true python backend/run_mock_backend.py
```

### Docker Issues

```bash
# Clean up containers
docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml down -v

# Rebuild images
docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml build --no-cache

# Check logs
docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml logs backend
```

## 📊 Test Results

Test results are stored in `integration_test_results/` with:
- JSON data (`estate_report.json`)
- HTML reports (`estate_report.html`)
- Individual product data in `products/` subdirectories

## 🤝 Contributing

1. Create a feature branch
2. Make changes
3. Run tests: `pytest tests/ -v`
4. Run integration test: `python final_integration_test.py`
5. Submit pull request

## 📄 License

MIT License - See LICENSE file for details

## 🔗 Resources

- [OpenAI API Documentation](https://platform.openai.com/docs)
- [eBay Developer Program](https://developer.ebay.com/)
- [Flutter Documentation](https://docs.flutter.dev/)
- [MCP Specification](https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/)

## 💡 Tips

- **Start in mock mode** to develop features without API costs
- **Use Docker for testing** to avoid environment issues
- **Check `.env.template`** for all configuration options
- **Run smoke tests** (`pytest -m smoke`) for quick validation
- **Use caching** (`CACHE_ENABLED=true`) to reduce API calls

---

Made with ❤️ by [Emmanuel Lawal](https://github.com/emmanuellawal)

#!/bin/bash
# PWA Setup and Test Script

echo "=================================="
echo "       ValueSnap - PWA Setup"
echo "=================================="
echo ""

# Check if virtual environment is activated
if [[ -z "$VIRTUAL_ENV" ]]; then
    echo "⚠️  Virtual environment not activated"
    echo "Activating venv..."
    source venv/bin/activate || {
        echo "❌ Failed to activate venv. Creating new one..."
        python3 -m venv venv
        source venv/bin/activate
    }
fi

echo "✅ Virtual environment: $VIRTUAL_ENV"
echo ""

# Step 1: Install backend dependencies
echo "📦 Step 1: Installing backend dependencies..."
pip install -q -r backend/requirements.txt
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "✅ Backend dependencies installed"
else
    echo "❌ Failed to install backend dependencies"
    exit 1
fi
echo ""

# Step 2: Initialize database
echo "💾 Step 2: Initializing database..."
python backend/database.py
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "✅ Database initialized"
else
    echo "❌ Failed to initialize database"
    exit 1
fi
echo ""

# Step 3: Check environment variables
echo "🔧 Step 3: Checking environment configuration..."
if [ -f .env ]; then
    echo "✅ .env file found"
    
    # Check for required variables
    if grep -q "SECRET_KEY" .env && grep -q "JWT_SECRET" .env; then
        echo "✅ Security keys configured"
    else
        echo "⚠️  Security keys not found in .env"
        echo "   The defaults will be used (OK for testing)"
    fi
    
    if grep -q "OPENAI_API_KEY" .env; then
        echo "✅ OpenAI API key configured"
    else
        echo "⚠️  OpenAI API key not found"
    fi
    
    if grep -q "EBAY_PROD_APP_ID" .env; then
        echo "✅ eBay API configured"
    else
        echo "⚠️  eBay API not configured"
    fi
else
    echo "❌ .env file not found!"
    exit 1
fi
echo ""

# Step 4: Start backend in background
echo "🚀 Step 4: Starting backend server..."
echo "   Backend will start on http://localhost:5000"
echo "   Press Ctrl+C to stop"
echo ""

python backend/run_backend_v1.py

#!/bin/bash
# eBay Listing Integration - Quick Setup Script
# Run this script to configure and test the implementation

echo "=========================================="
echo "eBay Listing Integration Setup"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""

# Step 1: Install Backend Dependencies
echo "📦 Step 1: Installing backend dependencies..."
cd backend
source ../venv/bin/activate
pip install cryptography
echo "✅ Backend dependencies installed"
echo ""

# Step 2: Generate Encryption Key
echo "🔐 Step 2: Generating encryption key..."
echo "Add this to your .env file:"
echo "EBAY_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(python -c 'from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())')"
echo ""
read -p "Press Enter after you've updated .env..."

# Step 3: Initialize Database
echo "🗄️  Step 3: Running database migration..."
python -c "from database import init_database; init_database()"
echo "✅ Database migration complete"
echo ""

# Step 4: Install Frontend Dependencies
echo "📱 Step 4: Installing frontend dependencies..."
cd ../appraisal-mobile
npm install expo-web-browser
echo "✅ Frontend dependencies installed"
echo ""

# Summary
echo "=========================================="
echo "✅ Setup Complete!"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""
echo "Next Steps:"
echo "1. Update EBAY_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI in .env"
echo "2. Register redirect URI in eBay Developer account"
echo "3. Start backend: python backend/run_backend_v1.py"
echo "4. Start frontend: npm start (in appraisal-mobile)"
echo "5. Test the OAuth flow in eBay Sandbox"
echo ""
echo "📚 See documentation/EBAY_LISTING_INTEGRATION.md for details"

#!/bin/bash
# Quick setup script for ebay-extract project

set -e

echo "🚀 Estate Inventory Extraction System - Setup"
echo "=============================================="
echo ""

# Check Python version
echo "📋 Checking prerequisites..."
python_version=$(python --version 2>&1 | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+')
if (( $(echo "$python_version < 3.10" | bc -l) )); then
    echo "❌ Python 3.10+ required (found $python_version)"
    exit 1
fi
echo "✅ Python $python_version"

# Check if venv exists
if [ ! -d "venv" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "📦 Creating virtual environment..."
    python -m venv venv
    echo "✅ Virtual environment created"
fi

# Activate venv
echo ""
echo "🔌 Activating virtual environment..."
source venv/bin/activate

# Install dependencies
echo ""
echo "📥 Installing dependencies..."
pip install -q --upgrade pip
pip install -q -r requirements.txt
pip install -q -r backend/requirements.txt
echo "✅ Dependencies installed"

# Setup .env if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f ".env" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "⚙️  Creating .env from template..."
    cp .env.template .env
    echo "✅ .env created (using mock mode by default)"
else
    echo ""
    echo "✅ .env already exists"
fi

# Verify setup
echo ""
echo "🧪 Verifying setup..."

# Test imports
python -c "import flask, openai, requests, yaml, pytest" 2>/dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "✅ All Python packages imported successfully"
else
    echo "❌ Some packages failed to import"
    exit 1
fi

# Check project structure
if [ -d "backend" ] && [ -d "src/estate_intake" ] && [ -d "tests" ]; then
    echo "✅ Project structure valid"
else
    echo "❌ Project structure incomplete"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "=============================================="
echo "✨ Setup complete! Next steps:"
echo ""
echo "1. Activate the virtual environment:"
echo "   source venv/bin/activate"
echo ""
echo "2. Run in spendless mock mode:"
echo "   python backend/run_mock_backend.py"
echo ""
echo "3. Or run the full integration test:"
echo "   python final_integration_test.py"
echo ""
echo "4. Or run automated tests:"
echo "   pytest tests/ -v -m smoke"
echo ""
echo "5. For Docker-based testing:"
echo "   docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build"
echo ""
echo "💡 Tip: Start with mock mode (USE_MOCK=true in .env)"
echo "    to develop without spending on API calls!"
echo ""

File: start-dev.sh

#!/bin/bash
# ValueSnap Development Startup Script
# Starts both backend and frontend servers concurrently

# Colors for terminal output
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
RED='\033[0;31m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Print header
echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"
echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}🚀 ValueSnap Development Environment${NC}"
echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"
echo ""

# Store PIDs for cleanup
BACKEND_PID=""
FRONTEND_PID=""

# Cleanup function
cleanup() {
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}${BOLD}🛑 Shutting down servers...${NC}"
    
    if [ ! -z "$BACKEND_PID" ]; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}   Stopping backend (PID: $BACKEND_PID)...${NC}"
        kill -TERM $BACKEND_PID 2>/dev/null
    fi
    
    if [ ! -z "$FRONTEND_PID" ]; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}   Stopping frontend (PID: $FRONTEND_PID)...${NC}"
        kill -TERM $FRONTEND_PID 2>/dev/null
    fi
    
    # Kill any remaining child processes
    pkill -P $$ 2>/dev/null
    
    echo -e "${GREEN}✅ Cleanup complete${NC}"
    exit 0
}

# Register cleanup on Ctrl+C and script exit
trap cleanup SIGINT SIGTERM EXIT

# Clear existing processes on required ports
echo -e "${BLUE}🧹 Clearing existing processes on ports...${NC}"
for port in 5000 8081 19000 19001 19002 19006; do
    if lsof -ti:$port > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}   Clearing port $port...${NC}"
        lsof -ti:$port | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
done
echo -e "${GREEN}✅ Ports cleared${NC}"
echo ""

# Check prerequisites
echo -e "${BLUE}🔍 Checking prerequisites...${NC}"

# Check Python
if ! command -v python &> /dev/null; then
    echo -e "${RED}❌ Python not found. Please install Python 3.8+${NC}"
    exit 1
fi
echo -e "${GREEN}✅ Python: $(python --version)${NC}"

# Check Node.js
if ! command -v node &> /dev/null; then
    echo -e "${RED}❌ Node.js not found. Please install Node.js 14+${NC}"
    exit 1
fi
echo -e "${GREEN}✅ Node.js: $(node --version)${NC}"

# Check npm
if ! command -v npm &> /dev/null; then
    echo -e "${RED}❌ npm not found. Please install npm${NC}"
    exit 1
fi
echo -e "${GREEN}✅ npm: $(npm --version)${NC}"

echo ""

# Check virtual environment
if [ ! -d "venv" ]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠️  Virtual environment not found. Creating one...${NC}"
    python -m venv venv
fi

# Verify sandbox mode before starting
echo -e "${BLUE}🔒 Verifying sandbox mode...${NC}"
SANDBOX_CHECK=$(grep "EBAY_USE_SANDBOX=true" .env 2>/dev/null)
if [ -z "$SANDBOX_CHECK" ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}❌ WARNING: EBAY_USE_SANDBOX not set to 'true' in .env${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}   Please run: ./verify_sandbox.sh${NC}"
    exit 1
fi
echo -e "${GREEN}✅ Sandbox mode: ACTIVE${NC}"

echo ""
echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"

# Start Backend
echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}📦 Starting Backend (Flask on port 5000)...${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}   Location: ./backend${NC}"

(
    cd backend
    source ../venv/bin/activate
    echo -e "${BLUE}   Activating virtual environment...${NC}"
    python run_backend.py 2>&1 | while IFS= read -r line; do
        echo -e "${BLUE}[BACKEND]${NC} $line"
    done
) &
BACKEND_PID=$!

echo -e "${GREEN}✅ Backend started (PID: $BACKEND_PID)${NC}"
echo ""

# Wait for backend to be ready
echo -e "${YELLOW}⏳ Waiting for backend to be ready...${NC}"
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -s http://localhost:5000/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✅ Backend is healthy and ready!${NC}"
        break
    fi
    sleep 1
    echo -n "."
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${RED}❌ Backend failed to start after 30 seconds${NC}"
        exit 1
    fi
done
echo ""

echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"

# Start Frontend
echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}📱 Starting Frontend (Expo)...${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}   Location: ./appraisal-mobile${NC}"

(
    cd appraisal-mobile
    
    # Check if node_modules exists
    if [ ! -d "node_modules" ]; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}   Installing dependencies...${NC}"
        npm install
    fi
    
    echo -e "${BLUE}   Starting Expo dev server...${NC}"
    npm start 2>&1 | while IFS= read -r line; do
        echo -e "${GREEN}[FRONTEND]${NC} $line"
    done
) &
FRONTEND_PID=$!

echo -e "${GREEN}✅ Frontend started (PID: $FRONTEND_PID)${NC}"
echo ""

# Wait a moment for Expo to initialize
sleep 3

echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"
echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}🎉 Both servers are running!${NC}"
echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"
echo ""
echo -e "${YELLOW}${BOLD}📍 Access Points:${NC}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}   Backend API:    ${BOLD}http://localhost:5000${NC}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}   Health Check:   ${BOLD}http://localhost:5000/health${NC}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}   Expo DevTools:  ${BOLD}http://localhost:19002${NC}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}   Expo App:       ${BOLD}http://localhost:19006${NC}"
echo ""
echo -e "${BLUE}${BOLD}📱 To view on your device:${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}   1. Install Expo Go app on your phone${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}   2. Scan the QR code that appears above${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}   3. Or press 'w' to open in web browser${NC}"
echo ""
echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"
echo ""
echo -e "${YELLOW}Press ${BOLD}Ctrl+C${NC}${YELLOW} to stop both servers${NC}"
echo ""

# Keep script running and monitor processes
while true; do
    # Check if backend is still running
    if ! kill -0 $BACKEND_PID 2>/dev/null; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${RED}❌ Backend process died unexpectedly!${NC}"
        break
    fi
    
    # Check if frontend is still running
    if ! kill -0 $FRONTEND_PID 2>/dev/null; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${RED}❌ Frontend process died unexpectedly!${NC}"
        break
    fi
    
    sleep 2
done

# If we get here, something crashed
exit 1

================
File: test_ebay_listing_integration.py

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
eBay Listing Integration Test
Tests the complete OAuth and listing workflow in sandbox mode
"""

import os
import sys
import json
import logging
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

# Add backend to path
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'backend'))

from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

def test_environment_setup():
    """Test 1: Verify environment variables are configured"""
    logger.info("\n" + "="*60)
    logger.info("TEST 1: Environment Configuration")
    logger.info("="*60)
    
    required_vars = [
        'EBAY_SANDBOX_APP_ID',
        'EBAY_SANDBOX_CERT_ID',
        'EBAY_SANDBOX_DEV_ID',
        'EBAY_USE_SANDBOX',
        'EBAY_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI',
        'EBAY_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY'
    ]
    
    results = {}
    for var in required_vars:
        value = os.getenv(var)
        results[var] = '✅ SET' if value else '❌ MISSING'
        if value:
            # Show partial value for security
            if 'KEY' in var or 'TOKEN' in var or 'CERT' in var:
                display = f"{value[:8]}...{value[-8:]}" if len(value) > 16 else "***"
            else:
                display = value
            logger.info(f"  {var}: {display}")
    
    sandbox_enabled = os.getenv('EBAY_USE_SANDBOX', '').lower() == 'true'
    logger.info(f"\n  Sandbox Mode: {'✅ ENABLED (SAFE)' if sandbox_enabled else '⚠️  DISABLED (DANGER)'}")
    
    all_set = all(v == '✅ SET' for v in results.values())
    return all_set and sandbox_enabled


def test_database_schema():
    """Test 2: Verify database has OAuth fields"""
    logger.info("\n" + "="*60)
    logger.info("TEST 2: Database Schema for OAuth")
    logger.info("="*60)
    
    try:
        from database import User, init_database
        
        # Initialize database
        init_database()
        
        # Check if User model has required OAuth fields
        oauth_fields = [
            'ebay_access_token',
            'ebay_refresh_token',
            'ebay_token_expires_at',
            'ebay_user_id',
            'ebay_linked_at'
        ]
        
        for field in oauth_fields:
            if hasattr(User, field):
                logger.info(f"  ✅ {field}: PRESENT")
            else:
                logger.error(f"  ❌ {field}: MISSING")
                return False
        
        logger.info("\n  Database schema is ready for OAuth tokens")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"  ❌ Database check failed: {e}")
        return False


def test_oauth_client():
    """Test 3: Test OAuth client initialization"""
    logger.info("\n" + "="*60)
    logger.info("TEST 3: eBay OAuth Client")
    logger.info("="*60)
    
    try:
        from ebay_oauth import EbayOAuthUser, get_ebay_oauth
        
        # Test sandbox mode
        oauth_client = get_ebay_oauth(use_sandbox=True)
        logger.info(f"  ✅ OAuth client initialized")
        logger.info(f"  App ID: {oauth_client.app_id[:10]}...")
        logger.info(f"  Auth URL: {oauth_client.auth_url}")
        logger.info(f"  Token URL: {oauth_client.token_url}")
        
        # Test authorization URL generation
        test_state = "test_state_12345"
        auth_url = oauth_client.get_authorization_url(test_state)
        logger.info(f"\n  Generated authorization URL:")
        logger.info(f"  {auth_url[:80]}...")
        
        # Verify URL contains required parameters
        required_params = ['client_id', 'redirect_uri', 'response_type', 'scope', 'state']
        for param in required_params:
            if param in auth_url:
                logger.info(f"  ✅ {param}: Present in URL")
            else:
                logger.error(f"  ❌ {param}: Missing from URL")
                return False
        
        # Test encryption (if configured)
        if oauth_client.cipher:
            test_token = "test_token_value_123"
            encrypted = oauth_client.encrypt_token(test_token)
            decrypted = oauth_client.decrypt_token(encrypted)
            
            if decrypted == test_token:
                logger.info(f"  ✅ Token encryption/decryption: WORKING")
            else:
                logger.error(f"  ❌ Token encryption/decryption: FAILED")
                return False
        else:
            logger.warning(f"  ⚠️  Token encryption not configured (EBAY_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY missing)")
        
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"  ❌ OAuth client test failed: {e}")
        import traceback
        traceback.print_exc()
        return False


def test_trading_api_client():
    """Test 4: Test Trading API client"""
    logger.info("\n" + "="*60)
    logger.info("TEST 4: eBay Trading API Client")
    logger.info("="*60)
    
    try:
        from ebay_trading_client import EbayTradingClient
        
        # Create client with fake token for structure test
        test_token = "fake_token_for_testing"
        trading_client = EbayTradingClient(test_token, use_sandbox=True)
        
        logger.info(f"  ✅ Trading client initialized")
        logger.info(f"  API URL: {trading_client.api_url}")
        logger.info(f"  API Version: {trading_client.api_version}")
        logger.info(f"  Site ID: {trading_client.site_id} (US)")
        
        # Test XML building
        listing_data = {
            'title': 'Test Item - DO NOT BID',
            'description': 'This is a test listing in sandbox mode',
            'price': 9.99,
            'category_id': '11700',
            'condition_id': '3000',
            'quantity': 1,
            'image_urls': ['https://example.com/test.jpg']
        }
        
        xml = trading_client._build_add_item_xml(listing_data)
        
        # Verify XML contains required elements
        required_xml = ['<Title>', '<Description>', '<StartPrice>', '<CategoryID>', '<ConditionID>']
        for element in required_xml:
            if element in xml:
                logger.info(f"  ✅ {element}: Present in XML")
            else:
                logger.error(f"  ❌ {element}: Missing from XML")
                return False
        
        logger.info(f"\n  XML Request Structure: VALID")
        logger.info(f"  XML Length: {len(xml)} bytes")
        
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"  ❌ Trading API test failed: {e}")
        import traceback
        traceback.print_exc()
        return False


def test_backend_endpoints():
    """Test 5: Verify backend endpoints exist"""
    logger.info("\n" + "="*60)
    logger.info("TEST 5: Backend OAuth Endpoints")
    logger.info("="*60)
    
    try:
        # Import Flask app
        sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'backend'))
        from run_backend_v1 import app
        
        # Get all routes
        routes = []
        for rule in app.url_map.iter_rules():
            if 'ebay' in rule.rule:
                routes.append({
                    'endpoint': rule.rule,
                    'methods': ','.join(rule.methods - {'HEAD', 'OPTIONS'})
                })
        
        logger.info(f"  Found {len(routes)} eBay-related endpoints:")
        for route in routes:
            logger.info(f"  ✅ {route['methods']:8s} {route['endpoint']}")
        
        # Check for required endpoints
        required_endpoints = [
            '/api/v1/ebay/auth/initiate',
            '/api/v1/ebay/auth/callback',
            '/api/v1/ebay/auth/status',
            '/api/v1/ebay/list'
        ]
        
        found_endpoints = [r['endpoint'] for r in routes]
        
        all_present = True
        logger.info("\n  Required endpoints:")
        for endpoint in required_endpoints:
            if endpoint in found_endpoints:
                logger.info(f"  ✅ {endpoint}")
            else:
                logger.error(f"  ❌ {endpoint}: MISSING")
                all_present = False
        
        return all_present
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"  ❌ Backend endpoint test failed: {e}")
        import traceback
        traceback.print_exc()
        return False


def test_frontend_service():
    """Test 6: Verify frontend service exists"""
    logger.info("\n" + "="*60)
    logger.info("TEST 6: Frontend eBay Service")
    logger.info("="*60)
    
    try:
        service_path = os.path.join(
            os.path.dirname(__file__),
            'appraisal-mobile',
            'src',
            'services',
            'ebayAuth.js'
        )
        
        if not os.path.exists(service_path):
            logger.error(f"  ❌ Service file not found: {service_path}")
            return False
        
        logger.info(f"  ✅ Service file exists: ebayAuth.js")
        
        with open(service_path, 'r') as f:
            content = f.read()
        
        # Check for required functions
        required_functions = [
            'checkEbayAuthStatus',
            'initiateEbayAuth',
            'handleEbayAuthCallback',
            'createEbayListing'
        ]
        
        logger.info("\n  Required functions:")
        for func in required_functions:
            if func in content:
                logger.info(f"  ✅ {func}")
            else:
                logger.error(f"  ❌ {func}: MISSING")
                return False
        
        # Check for API endpoints
        required_endpoints = [
            '/api/v1/ebay/auth/status',
            '/api/v1/ebay/auth/initiate',
            '/api/v1/ebay/auth/callback',
            '/api/v1/ebay/list'
        ]
        
        logger.info("\n  API endpoint calls:")
        for endpoint in required_endpoints:
            if endpoint in content:
                logger.info(f"  ✅ {endpoint}")
            else:
                logger.error(f"  ❌ {endpoint}: MISSING")
        
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"  ❌ Frontend service test failed: {e}")
        return False


def test_screen_implementation():
    """Test 7: Verify ListOnEbayScreen exists and is integrated"""
    logger.info("\n" + "="*60)
    logger.info("TEST 7: ListOnEbayScreen Implementation")
    logger.info("="*60)
    
    try:
        screen_path = os.path.join(
            os.path.dirname(__file__),
            'appraisal-mobile',
            'src',
            'screens',
            'ListOnEbayScreen.js'
        )
        
        if not os.path.exists(screen_path):
            logger.error(f"  ❌ Screen file not found: {screen_path}")
            return False
        
        logger.info(f"  ✅ Screen file exists: ListOnEbayScreen.js")
        
        with open(screen_path, 'r') as f:
            content = f.read()
        
        # Check for key features
        features = [
            ('Image Preview', 'Image'),
            ('Editable Title', 'TextInput'),
            ('Quick Sell Price', 'quicksell_price'),
            ('Patient Sell Price', 'patient_sell_price'),
            ('Custom Price', 'customPrice'),
            ('OAuth Integration', 'checkEbayAuthStatus'),
            ('List Button', 'createEbayListing')
        ]
        
        logger.info("\n  Key features:")
        for feature_name, search_term in features:
            if search_term in content:
                logger.info(f"  ✅ {feature_name}")
            else:
                logger.warning(f"  ⚠️  {feature_name}: Not detected (might use different implementation)")
        
        # Check for service import
        if 'ebayAuth' in content:
            logger.info(f"\n  ✅ eBay service imported")
        else:
            logger.error(f"\n  ❌ eBay service not imported")
            return False
        
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"  ❌ Screen test failed: {e}")
        return False


def print_summary(results):
    """Print test summary"""
    logger.info("\n" + "="*60)
    logger.info("TEST SUMMARY")
    logger.info("="*60)
    
    test_names = [
        "Environment Configuration",
        "Database Schema",
        "OAuth Client",
        "Trading API Client",
        "Backend Endpoints",
        "Frontend Service",
        "ListOnEbayScreen"
    ]
    
    for i, (name, passed) in enumerate(zip(test_names, results), 1):
        status = "✅ PASS" if passed else "❌ FAIL"
        logger.info(f"  Test {i}: {name:30s} {status}")
    
    total = len(results)
    passed = sum(results)
    percentage = (passed / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0
    
    logger.info(f"\n  Total: {passed}/{total} tests passed ({percentage:.0f}%)")
    
    if all(results):
        logger.info("\n" + "="*60)
        logger.info("🎉 ALL TESTS PASSED!")
        logger.info("="*60)
        logger.info("\neBay Listing Integration is COMPLETE and READY!")
        logger.info("\nNext Steps:")
        logger.info("  1. Start the backend: cd backend && source venv/bin/activate && python run_backend_v1.py")
        logger.info("  2. Start the frontend: cd appraisal-mobile && npm start")
        logger.info("  3. Test the OAuth flow with a sandbox test user")
        logger.info("  4. Create a test listing in the eBay sandbox")
        logger.info("\nReminder: ALWAYS keep EBAY_USE_SANDBOX=true during development!")
    else:
        logger.error("\n" + "="*60)
        logger.error("⚠️  SOME TESTS FAILED")
        logger.error("="*60)
        logger.error("\nPlease review the failed tests above and address any issues.")


def main():
    """Run all integration tests"""
    logger.info("="*60)
    logger.info("eBay Listing Integration Test Suite")
    logger.info("="*60)
    logger.info(f"Timestamp: {datetime.now().isoformat()}")
    logger.info(f"Working Directory: {os.getcwd()}")
    
    results = []
    
    # Run all tests
    results.append(test_environment_setup())
    results.append(test_database_schema())
    results.append(test_oauth_client())
    results.append(test_trading_api_client())
    results.append(test_backend_endpoints())
    results.append(test_frontend_service())
    results.append(test_screen_implementation())
    
    # Print summary
    print_summary(results)
    
    # Exit with appropriate code
    sys.exit(0 if all(results) else 1)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

File: test_pwa.py

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
ValueSnap - PWA Feature Test Suite
Tests the new v1 API, authentication, database, and PWA features
"""

import os
import sys
import requests
import json
import base64
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime

# Colors for output
GREEN = '\033[92m'
RED = '\033[91m'
YELLOW = '\033[93m'
BLUE = '\033[94m'
RESET = '\033[0m'

def print_header(text):
    print(f"\n{BLUE}{'='*60}{RESET}")
    print(f"{BLUE}{text.center(60)}{RESET}")
    print(f"{BLUE}{'='*60}{RESET}\n")

def print_success(text):
    print(f"{GREEN}✅ {text}{RESET}")

def print_error(text):
    print(f"{RED}❌ {text}{RESET}")

def print_info(text):
    print(f"{YELLOW}ℹ️  {text}{RESET}")

def print_result(key, value):
    print(f"   {key}: {value}")

# Test configuration
BACKEND_URL = "http://localhost:5000"
test_results = {
    'passed': 0,
    'failed': 0,
    'errors': []
}

def test_backend_running():
    """Test 1: Check if backend is running"""
    print_header("Test 1: Backend Server Status")
    
    try:
        response = requests.get(f"{BACKEND_URL}/health", timeout=5)
        if response.status_code == 200:
            print_success("Legacy backend is running")
            data = response.json()
            print_result("Status", data.get('status'))
            print_result("OpenAI configured", data.get('openai_configured'))
            print_result("eBay configured", data.get('ebay_configured'))
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return True
        else:
            print_error(f"Backend returned status code: {response.status_code}")
            test_results['failed'] += 1
            return False
    except requests.exceptions.ConnectionError:
        print_error("Backend is not running! Start it with: python backend/run_backend_v1.py")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return False
    except Exception as e:
        print_error(f"Error: {e}")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return False


def test_v1_health():
    """Test 2: Check v1 API health endpoint"""
    print_header("Test 2: API v1 Health Check")
    
    try:
        response = requests.get(f"{BACKEND_URL}/api/v1/health", timeout=5)
        if response.status_code == 200:
            print_success("API v1 health endpoint working")
            data = response.json()
            print_result("Status", data.get('status'))
            print_result("Version", data.get('version'))
            print_result("Timestamp", data.get('timestamp'))
            print_result("OpenAI", data.get('services', {}).get('openai'))
            print_result("eBay", data.get('services', {}).get('ebay'))
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return True
        else:
            print_error(f"v1 health check failed: {response.status_code}")
            test_results['failed'] += 1
            return False
    except Exception as e:
        print_error(f"Error: {e}")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return False


def test_guest_session():
    """Test 3: Create guest session"""
    print_header("Test 3: Guest Session Creation")
    
    try:
        response = requests.post(f"{BACKEND_URL}/api/v1/auth/guest", timeout=5)
        if response.status_code == 201:
            print_success("Guest session created successfully")
            data = response.json()
            print_result("Success", data.get('success'))
            print_result("User Type", data.get('user_type'))
            print_result("User ID", data.get('user_id', '')[:30] + '...')
            print_result("Token", data.get('token', '')[:40] + '...')
            print_result("Expires in", f"{data.get('expires_in')} seconds")
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return data.get('token')
        else:
            print_error(f"Guest session creation failed: {response.status_code}")
            print_error(response.text)
            test_results['failed'] += 1
            return None
    except Exception as e:
        print_error(f"Error: {e}")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return None


def test_authenticated_request(token):
    """Test 4: Test authenticated request with token"""
    print_header("Test 4: Authenticated Request")
    
    if not token:
        print_error("No token available, skipping test")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return False
    
    try:
        # Get a sample image
        image_path = Path("products/case-001-single/img-1.jpg")
        if not image_path.exists():
            print_error(f"Test image not found: {image_path}")
            test_results['failed'] += 1
            return False
        
        print_info(f"Loading test image: {image_path}")
        
        with open(image_path, "rb") as img_file:
            image_data = base64.b64encode(img_file.read()).decode('utf-8')
        
        print_info("Sending authenticated analysis request...")
        
        headers = {
            'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}',
            'Content-Type': 'application/json'
        }
        
        payload = {
            'image_base64': image_data
        }
        
        response = requests.post(
            f"{BACKEND_URL}/api/v1/analyze",
            headers=headers,
            json=payload,
            timeout=60
        )
        
        if response.status_code == 200:
            print_success("Authenticated analysis request successful")
            data = response.json()
            print_result("Success", data.get('success'))
            print_result("User ID", data.get('user_id', '')[:30] + '...')
            print_result("Timestamp", data.get('timestamp'))
            
            analysis = data.get('analysis', {})
            if analysis:
                print_info("Analysis results:")
                print_result("  Item Type", analysis.get('structured_data', {}).get('item_type'))
                print_result("  Description", (analysis.get('description', '')[:60] + '...'))
                print_result("  Quick-sell", analysis.get('quicksell_price'))
                print_result("  Fair price", analysis.get('patient_sell_price'))
            
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return True
        else:
            print_error(f"Authenticated request failed: {response.status_code}")
            print_error(response.text)
            test_results['failed'] += 1
            return False
            
    except Exception as e:
        print_error(f"Error: {e}")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return False


def test_rate_limiting():
    """Test 5: Test rate limiting"""
    print_header("Test 5: Rate Limiting")
    
    try:
        print_info("Checking rate limit headers...")
        response = requests.get(f"{BACKEND_URL}/api/v1/health")
        
        # Check for rate limit headers
        headers = response.headers
        if 'X-RateLimit-Limit' in headers or 'RateLimit-Limit' in headers:
            print_success("Rate limiting is enabled")
            for key, value in headers.items():
                if 'rate' in key.lower() or 'limit' in key.lower():
                    print_result(f"  {key}", value)
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return True
        else:
            print_info("Rate limiting headers not found (may be disabled)")
            print_info("This is OK for development")
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return True
            
    except Exception as e:
        print_error(f"Error: {e}")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return False


def test_database_initialization():
    """Test 6: Check database initialization"""
    print_header("Test 6: Database Initialization")
    
    try:
        # Add backend to path
        sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'backend'))
        
        from database import get_database_url, create_db_engine, Base
        
        db_url = get_database_url()
        print_info(f"Database URL: {db_url}")
        
        # Try to create engine
        engine = create_db_engine()
        print_success("Database engine created")
        
        # Check if tables exist
        from sqlalchemy import inspect
        inspector = inspect(engine)
        tables = inspector.get_table_names()
        
        if tables:
            print_success(f"Database initialized with {len(tables)} tables:")
            for table in tables:
                print_result(f"  - {table}", "✓")
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return True
        else:
            print_info("No tables found. Run: python backend/database.py")
            print_info("This will initialize the database")
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return True
            
    except Exception as e:
        print_error(f"Error: {e}")
        print_info("Run: python backend/database.py to initialize")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return False


def test_legacy_endpoints():
    """Test 7: Test legacy endpoint compatibility"""
    print_header("Test 7: Legacy Endpoint Compatibility")
    
    try:
        response = requests.get(f"{BACKEND_URL}/health", timeout=5)
        if response.status_code == 200:
            print_success("Legacy /health endpoint works")
            test_results['passed'] += 1
        else:
            print_error("Legacy /health endpoint failed")
            test_results['failed'] += 1
            return False
        
        # Test legacy /process endpoint (without actual image to save time)
        print_info("Legacy /process endpoint available")
        print_success("Backward compatibility maintained")
        
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print_error(f"Error: {e}")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return False


def test_cors_configuration():
    """Test 8: Check CORS configuration"""
    print_header("Test 8: CORS Configuration")
    
    try:
        response = requests.options(
            f"{BACKEND_URL}/api/v1/health",
            headers={'Origin': 'http://localhost:3000'}
        )
        
        cors_headers = {k: v for k, v in response.headers.items() 
                       if 'access-control' in k.lower()}
        
        if cors_headers:
            print_success("CORS is configured")
            for key, value in cors_headers.items():
                print_result(f"  {key}", value)
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return True
        else:
            print_info("CORS headers not found in OPTIONS response")
            print_info("Check CORS_ORIGINS in .env")
            test_results['passed'] += 1
            return True
            
    except Exception as e:
        print_error(f"Error: {e}")
        test_results['failed'] += 1
        return False


def print_summary():
    """Print test summary"""
    print_header("Test Summary")
    
    total = test_results['passed'] + test_results['failed']
    success_rate = (test_results['passed'] / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0
    
    print(f"Total tests: {total}")
    print(f"{GREEN}Passed: {test_results['passed']}{RESET}")
    print(f"{RED}Failed: {test_results['failed']}{RESET}")
    print(f"Success rate: {success_rate:.1f}%\n")
    
    if test_results['failed'] == 0:
        print(f"{GREEN}🎉 All tests passed! Your PWA backend is ready!{RESET}\n")
        print(f"{YELLOW}Next steps:{RESET}")
        print("  1. Start the frontend: cd appraisal-mobile && npm run web")
        print("  2. Test PWA installation in Chrome")
        print("  3. Test offline mode")
    else:
        print(f"{YELLOW}⚠️  Some tests failed. Check the errors above.{RESET}\n")
        print(f"{YELLOW}Make sure:{RESET}")
        print("  1. Backend is running: python backend/run_backend_v1.py")
        print("  2. Database is initialized: python backend/database.py")
        print("  3. All dependencies installed: pip install -r backend/requirements.txt")


def main():
    """Run all tests"""
    print_header("PWA Backend Test Suite")
    print(f"Testing backend at: {BACKEND_URL}")
    print(f"Started at: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}\n")
    
    # Run tests
    backend_running = test_backend_running()
    
    if backend_running:
        test_v1_health()
        token = test_guest_session()
        test_authenticated_request(token)
        test_rate_limiting()
        test_database_initialization()
        test_legacy_endpoints()
        test_cors_configuration()
    else:
        print_error("\nBackend is not running. Please start it first:")
        print_info("  cd backend && python run_backend_v1.py\n")
    
    # Print summary
    print_summary()
    
    # Return exit code
    return 0 if test_results['failed'] == 0 else 1


if __name__ == '__main__':
    sys.exit(main())

================
File: test-accessibility.js

/**
 * Playwright Accessibility Test
 * Analyzes aria-hidden conflicts and scroll behavior
 */

const { chromium } = require('playwright');

async function analyzeAccessibility() {
  console.log('🚀 Starting Playwright accessibility analysis...\n');
  
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ 
    headless: true,
    args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox']
  });
  const context = await browser.newContext({
    viewport: { width: 1280, height: 720 }
  });
  const page = await context.newPage();

  // Capture console messages to catch the aria-hidden warning
  const consoleMessages = [];
  page.on('console', msg => {
    consoleMessages.push({
      type: msg.type(),
      text: msg.text()
    });
  });

  try {
    console.log('📱 Navigating to Expo app at http://localhost:8081...\n');
    await page.goto('http://localhost:8081', { 
      waitUntil: 'networkidle',
      timeout: 30000 
    });
    
    // Wait for app to fully load
    await page.waitForTimeout(3000);
    
    console.log('='.repeat(60));
    console.log('📊 ANALYSIS REPORT');
    console.log('='.repeat(60));

    // 1. Find all elements with aria-hidden="true"
    const ariaHiddenElements = await page.$$eval('[aria-hidden="true"]', elements => {
      return elements.map(el => ({
        tagName: el.tagName,
        className: el.className,
        id: el.id || '(no id)',
        innerHTML: el.innerHTML.substring(0, 200) + (el.innerHTML.length > 200 ? '...' : ''),
        hasButtonDescendant: el.querySelector('button') !== null,
        hasFocusableDescendant: el.querySelector('button, input, a, [tabindex]') !== null
      }));
    });

    console.log('\n📌 ARIA-HIDDEN ELEMENTS:');
    console.log('-'.repeat(40));
    if (ariaHiddenElements.length === 0) {
      console.log('  No elements with aria-hidden="true" found');
    } else {
      ariaHiddenElements.forEach((el, i) => {
        console.log(`\n  [${i + 1}] <${el.tagName.toLowerCase()}>`);
        console.log(`      Class: ${el.className.substring(0, 80)}...`);
        console.log(`      ID: ${el.id}`);
        console.log(`      Has focusable descendants: ${el.hasFocusableDescendant ? '⚠️ YES' : '✅ No'}`);
        console.log(`      Has button descendants: ${el.hasButtonDescendant ? '⚠️ YES' : '✅ No'}`);
      });
    }

    // 2. Find the specific problematic pattern - buttons inside aria-hidden containers
    const problematicPatterns = await page.$$eval('[aria-hidden="true"]', elements => {
      return elements
        .filter(el => el.querySelector('button, input, a, [tabindex]:not([tabindex="-1"])'))
        .map(el => {
          const focusable = el.querySelector('button, input, a, [tabindex]:not([tabindex="-1"])');
          return {
            containerClass: el.className,
            focusableElement: {
              tagName: focusable.tagName,
              className: focusable.className,
              textContent: focusable.textContent?.substring(0, 50) || ''
            }
          };
        });
    });

    console.log('\n\n⚠️  PROBLEMATIC PATTERNS (aria-hidden with focusable descendants):');
    console.log('-'.repeat(40));
    if (problematicPatterns.length === 0) {
      console.log('  ✅ No problematic patterns detected');
    } else {
      problematicPatterns.forEach((p, i) => {
        console.log(`\n  [${i + 1}] Container: ${p.containerClass.substring(0, 60)}...`);
        console.log(`      Focusable child: <${p.focusableElement.tagName.toLowerCase()}>`);
        console.log(`      Child class: ${p.focusableElement.className.substring(0, 60)}...`);
        console.log(`      Content: "${p.focusableElement.textContent}"`);
      });
    }

    // 3. Analyze scroll behavior
    console.log('\n\n🔄 SCROLL ANALYSIS:');
    console.log('-'.repeat(40));
    
    const scrollableElements = await page.$$eval('*', elements => {
      return elements
        .filter(el => {
          const style = window.getComputedStyle(el);
          const overflowY = style.overflowY;
          const overflowX = style.overflowX;
          return (overflowY === 'scroll' || overflowY === 'auto' || 
                  overflowX === 'scroll' || overflowX === 'auto') &&
                 (el.scrollHeight > el.clientHeight || el.scrollWidth > el.clientWidth);
        })
        .map(el => ({
          tagName: el.tagName,
          className: el.className?.substring(0, 60) || '',
          scrollHeight: el.scrollHeight,
          clientHeight: el.clientHeight,
          scrollWidth: el.scrollWidth,
          clientWidth: el.clientWidth,
          isScrollable: el.scrollHeight > el.clientHeight || el.scrollWidth > el.clientWidth
        }));
    });

    if (scrollableElements.length === 0) {
      console.log('  No scrollable elements detected');
    } else {
      scrollableElements.forEach((el, i) => {
        console.log(`\n  [${i + 1}] <${el.tagName.toLowerCase()}>`);
        console.log(`      Class: ${el.className}...`);
        console.log(`      Scroll Height: ${el.scrollHeight}px (Client: ${el.clientHeight}px)`);
        console.log(`      Scroll Width: ${el.scrollWidth}px (Client: ${el.clientWidth}px)`);
        console.log(`      Can scroll: ${el.isScrollable ? '✅ Yes' : '❌ No'}`);
      });
    }

    // 4. Test actual scrolling - check both document and scrollable containers
    console.log('\n\n🖱️  SCROLL BEHAVIOR TEST:');
    console.log('-'.repeat(40));
    
    // Get all scrollable containers and test them
    const scrollTest = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      const results = [];
      
      // Test document scroll
      const docBefore = document.documentElement.scrollTop || document.body.scrollTop;
      document.documentElement.scrollTop = 100;
      document.body.scrollTop = 100;
      await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));
      const docAfter = document.documentElement.scrollTop || document.body.scrollTop;
      document.documentElement.scrollTop = 0;
      document.body.scrollTop = 0;
      
      results.push({
        element: 'Document',
        canScroll: docAfter !== docBefore,
        scrollHeight: document.documentElement.scrollHeight,
        clientHeight: document.documentElement.clientHeight
      });
      
      // Test scrollable containers
      const scrollables = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('*')).filter(el => {
        const style = window.getComputedStyle(el);
        const overflowY = style.overflowY;
        return (overflowY === 'scroll' || overflowY === 'auto') && 
               el.scrollHeight > el.clientHeight;
      });
      
      for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(scrollables.length, 3); i++) {
        const el = scrollables[i];
        const before = el.scrollTop;
        el.scrollTop = 100;
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));
        const after = el.scrollTop;
        el.scrollTop = 0;
        
        results.push({
          element: `Container ${i + 1} (${el.className.substring(0, 30)}...)`,
          canScroll: after !== before || after > 0,
          scrollHeight: el.scrollHeight,
          clientHeight: el.clientHeight
        });
      }
      
      return results;
    });
    
    scrollTest.forEach(test => {
      console.log(`\n  ${test.element}:`);
      console.log(`    Scrollable: ${test.canScroll ? '✅ Yes' : '❌ No'}`);
      console.log(`    Height: ${test.scrollHeight}px (visible: ${test.clientHeight}px)`);
    });

    // 5. Find all buttons and check if they're accessible
    console.log('\n\n🔘 BUTTON ACCESSIBILITY:');
    console.log('-'.repeat(40));
    
    const buttons = await page.$$eval('button', buttons => {
      return buttons.map(btn => {
        let isHidden = false;
        let hiddenAncestor = null;
        let current = btn.parentElement;
        
        while (current) {
          if (current.getAttribute('aria-hidden') === 'true') {
            isHidden = true;
            hiddenAncestor = current.className;
            break;
          }
          current = current.parentElement;
        }
        
        return {
          className: btn.className,
          textContent: btn.textContent?.substring(0, 30) || '',
          hasAriaHiddenAncestor: isHidden,
          hiddenAncestorClass: hiddenAncestor
        };
      });
    });

    const problematicButtons = buttons.filter(b => b.hasAriaHiddenAncestor);
    console.log(`  Total buttons: ${buttons.length}`);
    console.log(`  Buttons with aria-hidden ancestor: ${problematicButtons.length}`);
    
    if (problematicButtons.length > 0) {
      console.log('\n  ⚠️  PROBLEMATIC BUTTONS:');
      problematicButtons.forEach((btn, i) => {
        console.log(`\n    [${i + 1}] Class: ${btn.className.substring(0, 50)}...`);
        console.log(`        Text: "${btn.textContent}"`);
        console.log(`        Hidden ancestor: ${btn.hiddenAncestorClass?.substring(0, 50)}...`);
      });
    }

    // 6. Get the page HTML structure around the issue
    console.log('\n\n📋 RENDERED HTML STRUCTURE (key sections):');
    console.log('-'.repeat(40));
    
    const htmlStructure = await page.evaluate(() => {
      const getStructure = (el, depth = 0) => {
        if (depth > 5) return '';
        const indent = '  '.repeat(depth);
        const attrs = [];
        if (el.id) attrs.push(`id="${el.id}"`);
        if (el.className) attrs.push(`class="${el.className.substring(0, 40)}..."`);
        if (el.getAttribute('aria-hidden')) attrs.push(`aria-hidden="${el.getAttribute('aria-hidden')}"`);
        
        return `${indent}<${el.tagName.toLowerCase()} ${attrs.join(' ')}>`;
      };
      
      // Get structure around aria-hidden elements
      const ariaHidden = document.querySelectorAll('[aria-hidden="true"]');
      let structure = '';
      ariaHidden.forEach(el => {
        structure += getStructure(el, 0) + '\n';
        el.querySelectorAll('*').forEach(child => {
          if (child.tagName === 'BUTTON' || child.matches('[tabindex]')) {
            structure += getStructure(child, 1) + '\n';
          }
        });
      });
      return structure || 'No aria-hidden elements found';
    });
    
    console.log(htmlStructure);

    // 7. Console warnings
    console.log('\n\n📢 CONSOLE MESSAGES:');
    console.log('-'.repeat(40));
    const warnings = consoleMessages.filter(m => 
      m.text.includes('aria-hidden') || 
      m.text.includes('focus') ||
      m.type === 'warning' ||
      m.type === 'error'
    );
    
    if (warnings.length === 0) {
      console.log('  No relevant warnings captured');
    } else {
      warnings.forEach(w => {
        console.log(`  [${w.type.toUpperCase()}] ${w.text.substring(0, 200)}...`);
      });
    }

    // 8. Screenshot
    await page.screenshot({ path: '/home/elawa/projects/ebay-extract/accessibility-screenshot.png', fullPage: true });
    console.log('\n\n📸 Screenshot saved to: accessibility-screenshot.png');

    console.log('\n' + '='.repeat(60));
    console.log('✅ Analysis complete');
    console.log('='.repeat(60));

  } catch (error) {
    console.error('❌ Error during analysis:', error.message);
  } finally {
    await browser.close();
  }
}

analyzeAccessibility();

File: test-check-storage.js

/**
 * Check AsyncStorage for saved appraisals
 */

const { chromium } = require('playwright');

async function checkStorage() {
  console.log('🔍 Checking for saved appraisals...\n');
  
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ 
    headless: true,
    args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox']
  });
  const context = await browser.newContext({
    viewport: { width: 1280, height: 720 }
  });
  const page = await context.newPage();

  try {
    await page.goto('http://localhost:8081', { 
      waitUntil: 'networkidle',
      timeout: 30000 
    });
    await page.waitForTimeout(3000);

    // Check localStorage for appraisals
    const storageData = await page.evaluate(() => {
      const keys = Object.keys(localStorage);
      const data = {};
      
      keys.forEach(key => {
        try {
          const value = localStorage.getItem(key);
          if (value && value.length < 1000) {
            data[key] = value;
          } else if (value) {
            data[key] = `[${value.length} chars]`;
          }
        } catch (e) {
          data[key] = '[error reading]';
        }
      });
      
      // Specifically look for appraisals
      const appraisalsKey = '@valuesnap_appraisals';
      if (localStorage.getItem(appraisalsKey)) {
        try {
          const appraisals = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(appraisalsKey));
          data['__APPRAISALS__'] = {
            count: appraisals.length,
            items: appraisals.slice(0, 3).map(a => ({
              id: a.id,
              timestamp: a.timestamp,
              hasImage: !!a.imageUri,
              hasResult: !!a.result
            }))
          };
        } catch (e) {
          data['__APPRAISALS__'] = 'Error parsing';
        }
      }
      
      return data;
    });

    console.log('📦 LocalStorage contents:');
    console.log(JSON.stringify(storageData, null, 2));

    // Also check for any visible content
    const pageContent = await page.evaluate(() => {
      return {
        hasEmptyState: document.body.textContent.includes('No Appraisals Yet'),
        hasHistoryTitle: document.body.textContent.includes('Your saved appraisals'),
        visibleText: document.body.textContent.substring(0, 500)
      };
    });

    console.log('\n📄 Page state:');
    console.log(`   Empty state shown: ${pageContent.hasEmptyState}`);
    console.log(`   History title: ${pageContent.hasHistoryTitle}`);

  } catch (error) {
    console.error('❌ Error:', error.message);
  } finally {
    await browser.close();
  }
}

checkStorage();

File: test-history-scroll.js
